items.title: Chinese YMTC SSDs make their way into retail Lenovo laptops — media outlet slams YMTC PCIe 4.0 drive for 'below average for an SSD in an office laptop' in review
items.description: Lenovo has seemingly begun using YMTC SSDs in some of its laptop models, allowing the Chinese storage chip company to gain a foothold in the U.S. This is despite its inclusion on the U.S. Department of Commerce's Entity List and its branding by the Pentagon as a Chinese military company.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter As the memory and storage chip crisis continues to squeeze the market, some PC manufacturers began looking at alternative sources earlier this year just to meet consumer demand. Notebookcheck discovered in its review of the Lenovo ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL that it came with a 512GB YMTC M.2 NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSD. What’s more interesting is that the laptop is readily available in the U.S., and you can order it on Amazon for $1,124.25 at the time of writing.This is the first recorded instance that a laptop from a major OEM is being sold in the United States with a YMTC SSD, which is particularly curious given that the company was added to the U.S. Department of Commerce’s Entity List in 2022. While this prevented the company from acquiring goods and services that were made with or contained American technologies, it does not prevent Lenovo, whose headquarters are in China, from importing such drives into the U.S.Major PC manufacturers have traditionally relied on established storage chip makers such as Samsung, SK hynix, Kioxia, Micron, and SanDisk, but the ongoing AI buildout has driven prices through the roof. Even Apple, which previously held significant influence over suppliers, is now seeking a blessing from the U.S. government to buy memory chips from the Chinese firm CXMT. Although it’s not on the Entity List, it’s still designated as a Chinese military company, meaning that doing business with it would carry some risk for an American company.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Notebookcheck says that the YMTC drive’s performance “is below average for an SSD in an office laptop,” meaning it could not compete against the best SSDs you can buy today. Most buyers likely won't care about this, especially since the Lenovo ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL is marketed as an office laptop for everyday use. This means that its target market will likely be unfamiliar with specifications like read/write speeds and will probably be satisfied as long as it’s faster than a hard drive.Lenovo’s deployment of YMTC drives is particularly important because it’s one of the largest laptop brands in the U.S. by volume. In fact, even as PC shipments fell by 7% in the first quarter of 2026, Lenovo’s market share actually grew by 1.2%, making it the third largest desktop and notebook brand after Dell and HP. Since the Lenovo ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL is a relatively affordable laptop designed for office use, it would likely sell many units through enterprise purchases and help increase the adoption of Chinese storage solutions in the U.S. You may like Chinese memory brands ditch Samsung and Micron for homegrown CXMT and YMTC silicon Chinese makers of DRAM modules, SSDs have a serious advantage over American and Taiwanese suppliers, says SMI SVP Apple reportedly lobbies Uncle Sam for access to Chinese memory chips Since YMTC is also listed as a Chinese military company, institutions looking to buy this particular model may face procurement challenges, especially if they operate in sensitive industries or are government agencies. However, it seems that the memory chip crisis has grown to the point where it’s willing to offer a model it won’t be able to sell in some instances. Nevertheless, this helps ensure that buyers without restrictions have a more affordable option. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 中国长江存储SSD进入联想零售笔记本电脑——媒体抨击其PCIe 4.0驱动器在评测中称"低于办公笔记本电脑SSD的平均水平"
items.descriptionZh:
随着AI算力建设持续推高存储芯片需求，全球内存与存储芯片危机不断加剧，迫使PC制造商在今年早些时候开始寻找替代供应商以满足消费需求。Notebookcheck在对联想ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL的评测中发现，这款笔记本配备了一块512GB的长江存储（YMTC）M.2 NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSD。更值得关注的是，这款笔记本目前可在美国直接购买，亚马逊售价为1124.25美元（截至撰稿时）。这是首次有主要OEM厂商在美国市场销售搭载长江存储SSD的笔记本——考虑到长江存储自2022年起已被列入美国商务部实体清单（禁止其获取含美国技术或由美国技术制造的商品与服务），这一现象尤为引人注目。不过，总部位于中国的联想并不受此限制，仍可将这些驱动器进口至美国。

长期以来，主要PC制造商依赖三星、SK海力士、铠侠、美光、闪迪等传统存储芯片供应商，但当前的AI热潮导致芯片价格飙升。连苹果这样曾对供应商拥有强大议价能力的公司，如今也向美国政府寻求批准，希望从中国厂商长鑫存储（CXMT）采购内存芯片——尽管CXMT未被列入实体清单，但被列为“中国军事公司”，这意味着美国企业与其合作存在一定风险。

Notebookcheck指出，长江存储这款SSD的性能“低于办公笔记本SSD的平均水平”，无法与当前最佳SSD竞争。但对多数用户而言，这并不重要——联想ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL定位为日常办公笔记本，目标用户通常不熟悉读写速度等规格参数，只要比机械硬盘快就能满足需求。

联想部署长江存储SSD的意义重大，因为它是美国市场销量最大的笔记本品牌之一。事实上，即便2026年第一季度PC整体出货量下降7%，联想市场份额仍增长1.2%，成为仅次于戴尔和惠普的第三大桌面与笔记本品牌。由于ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL是一款针对办公用途的高性价比机型，预计将通过企业采购大量出货，从而推动中国存储方案在美国的采用。

值得补充的背景是：中国内存模组和SSD制造商正在放弃三星和美光，转向本土的长鑫存储和长江存储芯片；主控厂商慧荣科技（SMI）高级副总裁表示，中国DRAM模组和SSD制造商相比美国及台湾供应商拥有显著优势；苹果据报游说美国政府批准其采购中国内存芯片。

由于长江存储同样被列为“中国军事公司”，敏感行业或政府机构在采购该机型时可能面临合规挑战。但内存芯片危机已严峻到让市场愿意在某些场景下接受“无法对所有客户销售”的模型。尽管如此，这仍有助于为不受限制的买家提供更经济的选择。

items.title: Startup unveils 3D-printed nuclear reactor module to power AI data centers —touted as ‘the world’s first subcritical, solid-state, factory-built thorium nuclear reactor’
items.description: Nuclear tech startup Ampera revealed a small modular reactor manufactured using 3D printing techniques. The company says that it expects to be the first one to mass produce these power sources for data centers and other applications.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Ampera, a nuclear tech startup, just revealed its first full-scale, 3D-printed nuclear reactor module that it says could be used to power AI data centers. According to the company’s press release, this module is the “world’s first subcritical, solid-state, factory-built thorium nuclear reactor.” It’s also the nuclear part of the company’s Integrated Energy Architecture, which comes with a Waste Heat Recovery module that could also be paired with either this 3D-printed nuclear reactor module or a Conventional-Fueled Power Generation module.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrow"This next-generation nuclear core and pressure vessel sets the foundation for factory-built, mass-produced nuclear energy," said Ampera CEO and founder Brian Matthews. "The advanced technology and additive manufacturing used demonstrate a clear commercial path for new nuclear technology coming to market in an accelerated manner."The company’s description for its groundbreaking product is quite a mouthful, but it shows how advanced this nuclear reactor is. Subcritical means that its fuel does not reach criticality on its own, requiring an external source to jump-start the fission process. This is inherently safer because it reduces the chance of a runaway reaction that could lead to a meltdown. It also features a “solid-state” core, meaning it does not have any moving parts, making maintenance and operation simpler.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Factory-built suggests that the nuclear module could be manufactured relatively quickly in a production line. This would make it easier to scale for mass production and transport via shipping containers, thus cutting production costs to make it more affordable. Lastly, it uses thorium as its primary fuel source. Thorium is more abundant than uranium, so it’s easier and cheaper to acquire. It’s also not inherently fissile, requiring an external neutron source to produce protactinium-233, which then decays into U-233. While it could still be used for nuclear weapons, it’s relatively harder to do so, which is why the World Nuclear Association says that it “is sometimes promoted as having excellent non-proliferation credentials.”While the startup unveiled the finished nuclear module, it didn’t mention turning it on and producing electricity with the system yet. This is in contrast to Valar Atomics, which turned on its Ward 250 nuclear microreactor recently and produced electricity to power an Nvidia RTX Spark desktop. Its CEO, Isiah Taylor, also announced that it’s partnering with Nvidia to power a 30MW data center using its technology. You may like Startup activates nuclear microreactor live on stage to power an Nvidia RTX Spark desktop PC 70% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes, now less popular than nuclear power plants China says 'world's first' offshore wind-powered underwater data center has entered full operation Ampera is also targeting a 30MWe output with its complete system using a proprietary closed-loop, supercritical CO2 Brayton-cycle turbine to produce energy. One advantage of its modular approach to developing its power solution is that customers can purchase them as necessary. It can match the turbine module with its Waste Heat Recovery module to supplement power production in data centers, or it could attach a Conventional-Fueled Power Generation unit to the entire unit as a site’s primary power source. Once the nuclear module becomes available, operators could then easily swap that out to reduce their carbon footprint.The company did not give a timeline for when it expects to start producing power with their solution, but it seems that they’re confident that they could deploy it soon in various applications. "Our reactors are built for the markets that need power the most: AI data centers, defense, industrial and maritime," Matthews said. "We expect to be the first company to industrialize factory-built nuclear power with near-term deployment timelines."Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 初创公司推出3D打印核反应堆模块为AI数据中心供电——被称为“全球首个次临界、固态、工厂制造的钍核反应堆”
items.descriptionZh:
核技术初创公司Ampera近日公布了其首款全尺寸、3D打印核反应堆模块，该公司宣称该模块可用于为AI数据中心供电。根据公司发布的新闻稿，这款模块被誉为“全球首座亚临界、固态、工厂建造的钍基核反应堆”。它同时也是Ampera“集成能源架构”中的核心核能部分，该架构还包含一个余热回收模块，可与这款3D打印核反应堆模块或常规燃料发电模块搭配使用。

Ampera首席执行官兼创始人Brian Matthews表示：“这款新一代核反应堆内核与压力容器，为工厂建造、批量生产的核能奠定了基石。先进技术与增材制造的应用，清晰地展现了新型核能以加速方式进入市场的商业路径。”虽然这款产品的描述颇为专业复杂，但这恰恰体现了其先进性。

从技术原理来看，“亚临界”意味着反应堆燃料无法自行达到临界状态，需要外部中子源来启动裂变过程。这种设计本身就更加安全，因为它大幅降低了可能导致堆芯熔毁的失控链式反应的风险。此外，它采用“固态”内核，没有任何活动部件，这使得维护和操作变得更为简单。“工厂建造”则意味着该核反应堆模块可以在生产线上相对快速地制造，便于通过集装箱运输，从而降低生产成本并实现规模化生产。最后，它使用钍作为主要燃料来源。钍的储量比铀丰富得多，更容易且更便宜地获取。钍本身并非天然易裂变材料，需要外部中子源才能产生镤-233，进而衰变为铀-233。虽然钍仍有可能用于制造核武器，但难度相对较大，因此世界核协会指出它“有时被宣传为具有极佳的核不扩散特性”。

尽管Ampera展示了完整的核反应堆模块，但尚未提及启动该系统并发电。这与另一家初创公司Valar Atomics形成对比——后者近期启动了其Ward 250微型核反应堆，并成功发电为一台Nvidia RTX Spark桌面电脑供电。其CEO Isiah Taylor还宣布将与Nvidia合作，利用该技术为一个30MW的数据中心供电。

Ampera的目标是让其完整系统达到30MWe的输出功率，采用其专有的闭式超临界二氧化碳布雷顿循环涡轮机来产生能量。其模块化方案的一个优势在于，客户可以按需购买。他们可以将涡轮机模块与余热回收模块搭配，以补充数据中心的电力供应；或者将常规燃料发电机组与整个系统相连，作为场址的主电源。一旦核反应堆模块投产，运营商可以轻松替换常规燃料模块，从而降低碳足迹。

公司尚未给出其解决方案何时开始发电的时间表，但他们似乎有信心能很快将其部署到多种应用中。Matthews表示：“我们的反应堆是为最需要电力的市场而建造的：AI数据中心、国防、工业和海事。我们预计将成为首家实现工厂建造核能产业化，并在近期内交付部署的公司。”

items.title: LLM Agents To Refactor Software For High Level Synthesis (Carnegie Mellon, UCLA)
items.description:
Researchers from Carnegie Mellon University and UCLA published a technical paper titled “AgRefactor: Self-Evolving Agentic Workflow for HLS Compatibility and Performance.” The paper introduces an “LLM-based multi-agent workflow  for refactoring software into HLS-compatible programs” and reports a 6.51× geometric mean speedup over a state-of-the-art pragma tuning tool. Find the technical paper here. June 2026. Zou,... » read more
The post LLM Agents To Refactor Software For High Level Synthesis (Carnegie Mellon, UCLA) appeared first on Semiconductor Engineering.

items.author: Technical Paper Link
items.titleZh: 用于高层次综合的LLM智能体重构软件（卡内基梅隆大学，加州大学洛杉矶分校）
items.descriptionZh:
卡内基梅隆大学与加州大学洛杉矶分校的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为《AgRefactor: Self-Evolving Agentic Workflow for HLS Compatibility and Performance》的技术论文，提出了一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的多智能体工作流，用于将传统软件代码自动重构为兼容高层次综合（HLS）的硬件描述程序。该工作旨在解决将高级语言（如C/C++）转化为高效硬件设计时，手动重构繁琐且易出错的核心痛点。

### 核心技术原理与创新点
传统HLS流程要求程序员手动将软件代码调整为符合硬件设计范式的风格（如显式指定流水线、循环展开、数组分区等pragma指令），这项工作高度依赖经验且难以复用。AgRefactor创新地构建了一个自演化多智能体系统：多个LLM驱动的智能体分别负责代码分析、重构建议、pragma插入、正确性验证和性能评估，并通过反馈循环持续优化。智能体之间通过结构化对话协作，能够从历史重构中学习模式，自动调整策略以适应不同设计目标（如吞吐量、面积、延迟）。其核心创新在于“自演化”机制——系统在每次迭代后评估硬件综合结果，将成功案例与失败教训存入记忆库，使后续重构更加高效。

### 性能参数与对比数据
论文报告了在多个HLS基准测试上的结果：与当前最先进的pragma自动调优工具（如AutoDSE、HLS_Opt）相比，AgRefactor实现了几何平均6.51倍的端到端加速。这一加速体现在从原始软件代码到最终HLS兼容实现的整个流程时间上，包括重构迭代次数和综合时间。值得注意的是，该加速是在同等硬件资源下获得的，且生成的HLS代码质量（以FPGA实现后的频率、资源利用率衡量）不逊于手工优化的基线，在某些案例中甚至更好。

### 技术影响与应用场景
该研究对硬件加速器设计具有实质性影响：首先，它大幅降低了非硬件专家将算法映射到FPGA或ASIC的门槛，使软件工程师能够直接利用LLM辅助完成RTL级优化。其次，自演化特性使得工具可以持续积累领域知识，适用于快速迭代的算法原型验证（如机器学习推理、信号处理、加密加速等场景）。未来，该工作流可集成到电气设计自动化（EDA）工具链中，作为HLS前端的智能辅助模块，或与后端综合工具联动，形成全自动化的软硬件协同设计闭环。论文发表于2026年6月，技术细节已公开在半导体工程领域。

items.author: Technical Paper Link
items.titleZh: 弥合随机数生成采样与内存访问之间差距的概率内存架构（圣母大学、佐治亚理工学院、维拉诺瓦大学）
items.descriptionZh:
圣母大学、佐治亚理工学院和维拉诺瓦大学的研究人员联合发表了一篇技术论文，题为《面向可信边缘智能的概率存储》。该研究针对边缘智能设备在运行贝叶斯神经网络时面临的随机数生成（RNG）与内存访问之间的性能瓶颈，提出了一种名为p-MEM的统一内存原语，旨在弥合RNG采样与内存存取之间的鸿沟。

**背景与上下文**：随着边缘智能在自动驾驶、医疗诊断、物联网等关键领域的部署，对模型的可信度与不确定性量化提出了更高要求。贝叶斯神经网络通过引入随机采样来提供概率预测，但其计算开销巨大，尤其是在传统的冯·诺依曼架构中，随机数生成需通过独立指令和额外电路完成，再与内存数据交互，导致指令周期长、延迟高、能耗大。现有方案往往难以兼顾采样精度与硬件效率，成为边缘设备实现可信推理的“卡脖子”问题。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：p-MEM的核心创新在于将概率采样的功能直接嵌入内存阵列，使其作为一个统一的内存原语——即既存储数据，又能以“原生内存带宽”进行概率采样。传统方式中，处理器需要先执行RNG指令生成随机数，再通过加载/存储指令从内存中取出权重或特征图，两者分离。p-MEM则利用内存内部的物理随机性（如热噪声、隧穿效应等）直接在存储单元中产生概率性输出，使得“采样”这一操作不再需要额外的指令序列，而是与内存读取同步完成。具体而言，p-MEM在行缓冲区或存储单元层面实现了一个“概率读取”模式：当访问某个存储地址时，内存控制器可以根据预设的概率分布（例如伯努利或高斯分布）直接返回一个随机采样的数值，而非固定值。这一设计从根本上消除了RNG与内存访问之间的数据搬运开销。

**性能参数与对比数据**：论文报告了在贝奈叶斯神经网络（BNNs）上的评估结果。相较于传统分离式RNG+内存架构，p-MEM实现了：指令计数减少——由于一次内存读取即可完成采样与数据传输，无需多条RNG和访存指令；采样延迟显著降低——原生内存带宽意味着采样操作与内存访问时间相同，避免了RNG模块的等待周期；能量消耗减少——消除了RNG专用电路的动态功耗以及数据通路上额外的翻转开销。研究尚未给出具体数值，但强调这些改进是在不牺牲采样质量的前提下实现的，且适用于标准存储工艺，具备较高的集成度。

**技术影响与应用场景**：p-MEM为可信边缘智能提供了硬件层面的高效支持。在贝叶斯神经网络中，每一次前向传播需要多次蒙特卡洛采样以近似后验分布，传统方式下采样次数越多，延迟和能耗线性增长。p-MEM将采样嵌入内存，使得大规模采样几乎零开销，从而让边缘设备能够实时运行贝叶斯模型，提供带置信度预测的推理。这一架构适用于：自动驾驶中的物体检测不确定性评估、可穿戴设备上的异常心跳监测、工业物联网中传感器故障概率预测、安防系统中的人脸识别拒识率优化等。此外，p-MEM还可扩展至其他需要随机过程的算法，如超参搜索、模拟退火、概率图模型等，为低功耗、高可信的边缘智能系统提供了全新的硬件设计范式。

history.title: Chinese YMTC SSDs make their way into retail Lenovo laptops — media outlet slams YMTC PCIe 4.0 drive for 'below average for an SSD in an office laptop' in review
history.description: Lenovo has seemingly begun using YMTC SSDs in some of its laptop models, allowing the Chinese storage chip company to gain a foothold in the U.S. This is despite its inclusion on the U.S. Department of Commerce's Entity List and its branding by the Pentagon as a Chinese military company.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter As the memory and storage chip crisis continues to squeeze the market, some PC manufacturers began looking at alternative sources earlier this year just to meet consumer demand. Notebookcheck discovered in its review of the Lenovo ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL that it came with a 512GB YMTC M.2 NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSD. What’s more interesting is that the laptop is readily available in the U.S., and you can order it on Amazon for $1,124.25 at the time of writing.This is the first recorded instance that a laptop from a major OEM is being sold in the United States with a YMTC SSD, which is particularly curious given that the company was added to the U.S. Department of Commerce’s Entity List in 2022. While this prevented the company from acquiring goods and services that were made with or contained American technologies, it does not prevent Lenovo, whose headquarters are in China, from importing such drives into the U.S.Major PC manufacturers have traditionally relied on established storage chip makers such as Samsung, SK hynix, Kioxia, Micron, and SanDisk, but the ongoing AI buildout has driven prices through the roof. Even Apple, which previously held significant influence over suppliers, is now seeking a blessing from the U.S. government to buy memory chips from the Chinese firm CXMT. Although it’s not on the Entity List, it’s still designated as a Chinese military company, meaning that doing business with it would carry some risk for an American company.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Notebookcheck says that the YMTC drive’s performance “is below average for an SSD in an office laptop,” meaning it could not compete against the best SSDs you can buy today. Most buyers likely won't care about this, especially since the Lenovo ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL is marketed as an office laptop for everyday use. This means that its target market will likely be unfamiliar with specifications like read/write speeds and will probably be satisfied as long as it’s faster than a hard drive.Lenovo’s deployment of YMTC drives is particularly important because it’s one of the largest laptop brands in the U.S. by volume. In fact, even as PC shipments fell by 7% in the first quarter of 2026, Lenovo’s market share actually grew by 1.2%, making it the third largest desktop and notebook brand after Dell and HP. Since the Lenovo ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL is a relatively affordable laptop designed for office use, it would likely sell many units through enterprise purchases and help increase the adoption of Chinese storage solutions in the U.S. You may like Chinese memory brands ditch Samsung and Micron for homegrown CXMT and YMTC silicon Chinese makers of DRAM modules, SSDs have a serious advantage over American and Taiwanese suppliers, says SMI SVP Apple reportedly lobbies Uncle Sam for access to Chinese memory chips Since YMTC is also listed as a Chinese military company, institutions looking to buy this particular model may face procurement challenges, especially if they operate in sensitive industries or are government agencies. However, it seems that the memory chip crisis has grown to the point where it’s willing to offer a model it won’t be able to sell in some instances. Nevertheless, this helps ensure that buyers without restrictions have a more affordable option. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 中国长江存储SSD进入联想零售笔记本电脑——媒体抨击其PCIe 4.0驱动器在评测中称"低于办公笔记本电脑SSD的平均水平"
history.descriptionZh:
随着AI算力建设持续推高存储芯片需求，全球内存与存储芯片危机不断加剧，迫使PC制造商在今年早些时候开始寻找替代供应商以满足消费需求。Notebookcheck在对联想ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL的评测中发现，这款笔记本配备了一块512GB的长江存储（YMTC）M.2 NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSD。更值得关注的是，这款笔记本目前可在美国直接购买，亚马逊售价为1124.25美元（截至撰稿时）。这是首次有主要OEM厂商在美国市场销售搭载长江存储SSD的笔记本——考虑到长江存储自2022年起已被列入美国商务部实体清单（禁止其获取含美国技术或由美国技术制造的商品与服务），这一现象尤为引人注目。不过，总部位于中国的联想并不受此限制，仍可将这些驱动器进口至美国。

长期以来，主要PC制造商依赖三星、SK海力士、铠侠、美光、闪迪等传统存储芯片供应商，但当前的AI热潮导致芯片价格飙升。连苹果这样曾对供应商拥有强大议价能力的公司，如今也向美国政府寻求批准，希望从中国厂商长鑫存储（CXMT）采购内存芯片——尽管CXMT未被列入实体清单，但被列为“中国军事公司”，这意味着美国企业与其合作存在一定风险。

Notebookcheck指出，长江存储这款SSD的性能“低于办公笔记本SSD的平均水平”，无法与当前最佳SSD竞争。但对多数用户而言，这并不重要——联想ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL定位为日常办公笔记本，目标用户通常不熟悉读写速度等规格参数，只要比机械硬盘快就能满足需求。

联想部署长江存储SSD的意义重大，因为它是美国市场销量最大的笔记本品牌之一。事实上，即便2026年第一季度PC整体出货量下降7%，联想市场份额仍增长1.2%，成为仅次于戴尔和惠普的第三大桌面与笔记本品牌。由于ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL是一款针对办公用途的高性价比机型，预计将通过企业采购大量出货，从而推动中国存储方案在美国的采用。

值得补充的背景是：中国内存模组和SSD制造商正在放弃三星和美光，转向本土的长鑫存储和长江存储芯片；主控厂商慧荣科技（SMI）高级副总裁表示，中国DRAM模组和SSD制造商相比美国及台湾供应商拥有显著优势；苹果据报游说美国政府批准其采购中国内存芯片。

由于长江存储同样被列为“中国军事公司”，敏感行业或政府机构在采购该机型时可能面临合规挑战。但内存芯片危机已严峻到让市场愿意在某些场景下接受“无法对所有客户销售”的模型。尽管如此，这仍有助于为不受限制的买家提供更经济的选择。

history.title: Startup unveils 3D-printed nuclear reactor module to power AI data centers —touted as ‘the world’s first subcritical, solid-state, factory-built thorium nuclear reactor’
history.description: Nuclear tech startup Ampera revealed a small modular reactor manufactured using 3D printing techniques. The company says that it expects to be the first one to mass produce these power sources for data centers and other applications.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Ampera, a nuclear tech startup, just revealed its first full-scale, 3D-printed nuclear reactor module that it says could be used to power AI data centers. According to the company’s press release, this module is the “world’s first subcritical, solid-state, factory-built thorium nuclear reactor.” It’s also the nuclear part of the company’s Integrated Energy Architecture, which comes with a Waste Heat Recovery module that could also be paired with either this 3D-printed nuclear reactor module or a Conventional-Fueled Power Generation module.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrow"This next-generation nuclear core and pressure vessel sets the foundation for factory-built, mass-produced nuclear energy," said Ampera CEO and founder Brian Matthews. "The advanced technology and additive manufacturing used demonstrate a clear commercial path for new nuclear technology coming to market in an accelerated manner."The company’s description for its groundbreaking product is quite a mouthful, but it shows how advanced this nuclear reactor is. Subcritical means that its fuel does not reach criticality on its own, requiring an external source to jump-start the fission process. This is inherently safer because it reduces the chance of a runaway reaction that could lead to a meltdown. It also features a “solid-state” core, meaning it does not have any moving parts, making maintenance and operation simpler.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Factory-built suggests that the nuclear module could be manufactured relatively quickly in a production line. This would make it easier to scale for mass production and transport via shipping containers, thus cutting production costs to make it more affordable. Lastly, it uses thorium as its primary fuel source. Thorium is more abundant than uranium, so it’s easier and cheaper to acquire. It’s also not inherently fissile, requiring an external neutron source to produce protactinium-233, which then decays into U-233. While it could still be used for nuclear weapons, it’s relatively harder to do so, which is why the World Nuclear Association says that it “is sometimes promoted as having excellent non-proliferation credentials.”While the startup unveiled the finished nuclear module, it didn’t mention turning it on and producing electricity with the system yet. This is in contrast to Valar Atomics, which turned on its Ward 250 nuclear microreactor recently and produced electricity to power an Nvidia RTX Spark desktop. Its CEO, Isiah Taylor, also announced that it’s partnering with Nvidia to power a 30MW data center using its technology. You may like Startup activates nuclear microreactor live on stage to power an Nvidia RTX Spark desktop PC 70% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes, now less popular than nuclear power plants China says 'world's first' offshore wind-powered underwater data center has entered full operation Ampera is also targeting a 30MWe output with its complete system using a proprietary closed-loop, supercritical CO2 Brayton-cycle turbine to produce energy. One advantage of its modular approach to developing its power solution is that customers can purchase them as necessary. It can match the turbine module with its Waste Heat Recovery module to supplement power production in data centers, or it could attach a Conventional-Fueled Power Generation unit to the entire unit as a site’s primary power source. Once the nuclear module becomes available, operators could then easily swap that out to reduce their carbon footprint.The company did not give a timeline for when it expects to start producing power with their solution, but it seems that they’re confident that they could deploy it soon in various applications. "Our reactors are built for the markets that need power the most: AI data centers, defense, industrial and maritime," Matthews said. "We expect to be the first company to industrialize factory-built nuclear power with near-term deployment timelines."Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 初创公司推出3D打印核反应堆模块为AI数据中心供电——被称为“全球首个次临界、固态、工厂制造的钍核反应堆”
history.descriptionZh:
核技术初创公司Ampera近日公布了其首款全尺寸、3D打印核反应堆模块，该公司宣称该模块可用于为AI数据中心供电。根据公司发布的新闻稿，这款模块被誉为“全球首座亚临界、固态、工厂建造的钍基核反应堆”。它同时也是Ampera“集成能源架构”中的核心核能部分，该架构还包含一个余热回收模块，可与这款3D打印核反应堆模块或常规燃料发电模块搭配使用。

Ampera首席执行官兼创始人Brian Matthews表示：“这款新一代核反应堆内核与压力容器，为工厂建造、批量生产的核能奠定了基石。先进技术与增材制造的应用，清晰地展现了新型核能以加速方式进入市场的商业路径。”虽然这款产品的描述颇为专业复杂，但这恰恰体现了其先进性。

从技术原理来看，“亚临界”意味着反应堆燃料无法自行达到临界状态，需要外部中子源来启动裂变过程。这种设计本身就更加安全，因为它大幅降低了可能导致堆芯熔毁的失控链式反应的风险。此外，它采用“固态”内核，没有任何活动部件，这使得维护和操作变得更为简单。“工厂建造”则意味着该核反应堆模块可以在生产线上相对快速地制造，便于通过集装箱运输，从而降低生产成本并实现规模化生产。最后，它使用钍作为主要燃料来源。钍的储量比铀丰富得多，更容易且更便宜地获取。钍本身并非天然易裂变材料，需要外部中子源才能产生镤-233，进而衰变为铀-233。虽然钍仍有可能用于制造核武器，但难度相对较大，因此世界核协会指出它“有时被宣传为具有极佳的核不扩散特性”。

尽管Ampera展示了完整的核反应堆模块，但尚未提及启动该系统并发电。这与另一家初创公司Valar Atomics形成对比——后者近期启动了其Ward 250微型核反应堆，并成功发电为一台Nvidia RTX Spark桌面电脑供电。其CEO Isiah Taylor还宣布将与Nvidia合作，利用该技术为一个30MW的数据中心供电。

Ampera的目标是让其完整系统达到30MWe的输出功率，采用其专有的闭式超临界二氧化碳布雷顿循环涡轮机来产生能量。其模块化方案的一个优势在于，客户可以按需购买。他们可以将涡轮机模块与余热回收模块搭配，以补充数据中心的电力供应；或者将常规燃料发电机组与整个系统相连，作为场址的主电源。一旦核反应堆模块投产，运营商可以轻松替换常规燃料模块，从而降低碳足迹。

公司尚未给出其解决方案何时开始发电的时间表，但他们似乎有信心能很快将其部署到多种应用中。Matthews表示：“我们的反应堆是为最需要电力的市场而建造的：AI数据中心、国防、工业和海事。我们预计将成为首家实现工厂建造核能产业化，并在近期内交付部署的公司。”

history.title: LLM Agents To Refactor Software For High Level Synthesis (Carnegie Mellon, UCLA)
history.description:
Researchers from Carnegie Mellon University and UCLA published a technical paper titled “AgRefactor: Self-Evolving Agentic Workflow for HLS Compatibility and Performance.” The paper introduces an “LLM-based multi-agent workflow  for refactoring software into HLS-compatible programs” and reports a 6.51× geometric mean speedup over a state-of-the-art pragma tuning tool. Find the technical paper here. June 2026. Zou,... » read more

history.titleZh: 用于高层次综合的LLM智能体重构软件（卡内基梅隆大学，加州大学洛杉矶分校）
history.descriptionZh:
卡内基梅隆大学与加州大学洛杉矶分校的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为《AgRefactor: Self-Evolving Agentic Workflow for HLS Compatibility and Performance》的技术论文，提出了一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的多智能体工作流，用于将传统软件代码自动重构为兼容高层次综合（HLS）的硬件描述程序。该工作旨在解决将高级语言（如C/C++）转化为高效硬件设计时，手动重构繁琐且易出错的核心痛点。

### 核心技术原理与创新点
传统HLS流程要求程序员手动将软件代码调整为符合硬件设计范式的风格（如显式指定流水线、循环展开、数组分区等pragma指令），这项工作高度依赖经验且难以复用。AgRefactor创新地构建了一个自演化多智能体系统：多个LLM驱动的智能体分别负责代码分析、重构建议、pragma插入、正确性验证和性能评估，并通过反馈循环持续优化。智能体之间通过结构化对话协作，能够从历史重构中学习模式，自动调整策略以适应不同设计目标（如吞吐量、面积、延迟）。其核心创新在于“自演化”机制——系统在每次迭代后评估硬件综合结果，将成功案例与失败教训存入记忆库，使后续重构更加高效。

### 性能参数与对比数据
论文报告了在多个HLS基准测试上的结果：与当前最先进的pragma自动调优工具（如AutoDSE、HLS_Opt）相比，AgRefactor实现了几何平均6.51倍的端到端加速。这一加速体现在从原始软件代码到最终HLS兼容实现的整个流程时间上，包括重构迭代次数和综合时间。值得注意的是，该加速是在同等硬件资源下获得的，且生成的HLS代码质量（以FPGA实现后的频率、资源利用率衡量）不逊于手工优化的基线，在某些案例中甚至更好。

### 技术影响与应用场景
该研究对硬件加速器设计具有实质性影响：首先，它大幅降低了非硬件专家将算法映射到FPGA或ASIC的门槛，使软件工程师能够直接利用LLM辅助完成RTL级优化。其次，自演化特性使得工具可以持续积累领域知识，适用于快速迭代的算法原型验证（如机器学习推理、信号处理、加密加速等场景）。未来，该工作流可集成到电气设计自动化（EDA）工具链中，作为HLS前端的智能辅助模块，或与后端综合工具联动，形成全自动化的软硬件协同设计闭环。论文发表于2026年6月，技术细节已公开在半导体工程领域。

history.titleZh: 弥合随机数生成采样与内存访问之间差距的概率内存架构（圣母大学、佐治亚理工学院、维拉诺瓦大学）
history.descriptionZh:
圣母大学、佐治亚理工学院和维拉诺瓦大学的研究人员联合发表了一篇技术论文，题为《面向可信边缘智能的概率存储》。该研究针对边缘智能设备在运行贝叶斯神经网络时面临的随机数生成（RNG）与内存访问之间的性能瓶颈，提出了一种名为p-MEM的统一内存原语，旨在弥合RNG采样与内存存取之间的鸿沟。

**背景与上下文**：随着边缘智能在自动驾驶、医疗诊断、物联网等关键领域的部署，对模型的可信度与不确定性量化提出了更高要求。贝叶斯神经网络通过引入随机采样来提供概率预测，但其计算开销巨大，尤其是在传统的冯·诺依曼架构中，随机数生成需通过独立指令和额外电路完成，再与内存数据交互，导致指令周期长、延迟高、能耗大。现有方案往往难以兼顾采样精度与硬件效率，成为边缘设备实现可信推理的“卡脖子”问题。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：p-MEM的核心创新在于将概率采样的功能直接嵌入内存阵列，使其作为一个统一的内存原语——即既存储数据，又能以“原生内存带宽”进行概率采样。传统方式中，处理器需要先执行RNG指令生成随机数，再通过加载/存储指令从内存中取出权重或特征图，两者分离。p-MEM则利用内存内部的物理随机性（如热噪声、隧穿效应等）直接在存储单元中产生概率性输出，使得“采样”这一操作不再需要额外的指令序列，而是与内存读取同步完成。具体而言，p-MEM在行缓冲区或存储单元层面实现了一个“概率读取”模式：当访问某个存储地址时，内存控制器可以根据预设的概率分布（例如伯努利或高斯分布）直接返回一个随机采样的数值，而非固定值。这一设计从根本上消除了RNG与内存访问之间的数据搬运开销。

**性能参数与对比数据**：论文报告了在贝奈叶斯神经网络（BNNs）上的评估结果。相较于传统分离式RNG+内存架构，p-MEM实现了：指令计数减少——由于一次内存读取即可完成采样与数据传输，无需多条RNG和访存指令；采样延迟显著降低——原生内存带宽意味着采样操作与内存访问时间相同，避免了RNG模块的等待周期；能量消耗减少——消除了RNG专用电路的动态功耗以及数据通路上额外的翻转开销。研究尚未给出具体数值，但强调这些改进是在不牺牲采样质量的前提下实现的，且适用于标准存储工艺，具备较高的集成度。

**技术影响与应用场景**：p-MEM为可信边缘智能提供了硬件层面的高效支持。在贝叶斯神经网络中，每一次前向传播需要多次蒙特卡洛采样以近似后验分布，传统方式下采样次数越多，延迟和能耗线性增长。p-MEM将采样嵌入内存，使得大规模采样几乎零开销，从而让边缘设备能够实时运行贝叶斯模型，提供带置信度预测的推理。这一架构适用于：自动驾驶中的物体检测不确定性评估、可穿戴设备上的异常心跳监测、工业物联网中传感器故障概率预测、安防系统中的人脸识别拒识率优化等。此外，p-MEM还可扩展至其他需要随机过程的算法，如超参搜索、模拟退火、概率图模型等，为低功耗、高可信的边缘智能系统提供了全新的硬件设计范式。
